title: Add find-claude-session script: search ~/.claude sessions by substring
description: Add find-claude-session, a stdlib-only Python script that searches ~/.claude/projects/**/<uuid>.jsonl for a substring and prints matching session IDs. Default scope is the current project (cwd → mangled dir under ~/.claude/projects/); -g/--global searches all projects. Default search domain is user-typed messages; -a/--all includes assistant text and tool inputs/outputs. Output is git-log-style (timestamp, project, session-id, match count + snippet); -q/--quiet prints only session IDs for piping to e.g. `claude --resume`. Lives alongside the other beads-utils helpers despite not using bd — this repo is the catch-all for dev utilities in this workspace.
design: Key decisions: (1) Default scope = cwd's mangled dir under ~/.claude/projects/ (re.sub('[/.]', '-', cwd)), with fallback scanning all projects filtered by embedded 'cwd' field. -g/--global searches all projects. (2) Default search domain = type=='user' entries with string or text-block content only (excludes tool-result-carrying user entries to avoid Bash/tool output noise). -a/--all adds assistant text, thinking, tool_use inputs (json.dumps), and tool_result content. (3) Output: git-log-style with full 36-char UUID (paste-ready for claude --resume), up to 3 snippets per session (~80 chars, whitespace-collapsed, … ellipsis), newest-first by file mtime. -q emits only UUIDs. (4) Shares 'less' pager tooling with bd-log via new paged_output() context manager in bdutils.py; bd-log refactored to use it (removes standalone open_pager + ~15 lines of pager boilerplate in main).

title: Add bd-log utility: show recently closed beads
description: bd lacks a repo-wide 'git log'-style view of recent activity across all issues. bd history works per-id; bd list can filter closed but doesn't render as a timeline with dates/actors/reasons. Add a bd-log Python script that wraps 'bd list --status=closed --sort=closed --reverse --json' and renders a compact, log-style view.
design: v1 scope: closures only (user explicitly accepted 'at least' bar). Defaults: last 10 events; no day filter. Data source: 'bd list --status=closed --sort=closed --reverse --limit=N --json' — fast, supported, avoids unreliable .beads/interactions.jsonl (sparse in practice) and embedded-Dolt SQL plumbing. Conventions per CLAUDE.md: Python 3 stdlib, argparse RawDescriptionHelpFormatter, reuse bdutils.resolve_project_path and bdutils.error. Broader-activity feed deferred — revisit only if closures-only proves insufficient in daily use.
acceptance_criteria: bd-log runs from repo dir; shows last 10 closures with close date, id, actor, title, priority/type, close reason; --limit/-n flag; --since YYYY-MM-DD flag passed through as --closed-after; stdlib-only; manual run on this repo produces sensible output.
notes: Output format: 3-line-per-issue block (status/date/id/priority/type header; title; close reason with actor). Rationale: close reason is the most interesting field and one-line truncates it. --since: pass user value through verbatim to bd's --closed-after (no extra parsing layer; bd validates YYYY-MM-DD/RFC3339). Both confirmed by user.
close_reason: bd-log v2: git-style auto-pager ($PAGER or 'less -FRX'; --no-pager to disable); default -n 0 (unlimited, like git log). Manually verified with user in fresh terminal — short output looks direct, long output pages cleanly. Root-caused a separate LESS=-R oh-my-zsh default (lib/misc.zsh) and fixed it via a new .zshenv-layer in core-personal-files.

title: Rewrite dolt-remote-check in Python; unify conventions
description: The two utility scripts in this repo were written on different days with different tooling. bd-export-csv is Python (argparse, stdlib json, error: prefix), dolt-remote-check is Bash (manual case, python3 -c JSON one-liners, Error: prefix, banner-style help). Port dolt-remote-check to Python so both scripts share the same surface, eliminate the embedded python3 -c calls for JSON reading, and normalize CLI/help/error conventions across the repo. Also update CLAUDE.md to nail down error: (lowercase) as the one convention, replacing 'error: / Error:'.
design: Direction A (full Python rewrite). Shared bdutils.py module replaces the 'single self-contained executable' ethos — user explicitly accepted 'time for a new ethos' to favor DRY. Each script gets a 2-line symlink-safe prelude (sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent))) before 'from bdutils import ...' so ~/bin/ symlinks still work. Error prefix standardized to lowercase 'error:' matching Python convention; CLAUDE.md updated to drop '/ Error:' variant. Behavior change during port: OUT OF SYNC now exits 1 (was 0 in bash) so CI can gate on it. Script names unchanged (bd-export-csv vs dolt-remote-check) — different concerns, different prefixes. Old bash retained as dolt-remote-check.bash briefly for user smoke-test, then deleted in follow-up commit.
acceptance_criteria: dolt-remote-check is a Python 3 stdlib-only script; argparse with help examples; error: lowercase convention; same project-path handling as bd-export-csv (Path.expanduser().resolve(), no cd); output sections preserved; manual run verified on this repo; CLAUDE.md updated.
notes: Correction to design: ~/bin/ does NOT use symlinks — it holds forwarding stubs (commit edaa8dd in ~/bin repo) that print 'moved to github.com/deg/beads-utils' and exit 1. Scripts run from the repo directory, where Python's default sys.path[0] resolves the sibling bdutils.py. No symlink-safe prelude needed. Plain 'from bdutils import ...' suffices.
close_reason: Ported dolt-remote-check to Python; extracted bdutils.py shared helpers; DRY'd bd-export-csv; updated CLAUDE.md conventions; deleted old bash. Manual smoke-test passed (Python output byte-identical to bash; OUT OF SYNC now exits 1 as intended).

value: Do not run 'git commit' or 'bd close <id>' until the user has explicitly tested the feature and confirmed it works. Why: The /start-task and /finalize directives treat user-verification as a gate before finalization. Committing/closing first means the user has to ask for a revert when a bug is found. How to apply: After writing code, present (a) file locations, (b) how to test, (c) expected output. Then WAIT. Only commit + close bead after the user says 'looks good' / 'ship it' / equivalent. Applies even in Auto mode — Auto mode minimizes interruptions, it does not skip user-verification gates.
